channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Ghibli-Style AI Art Raises Ethical Concerns - The Associated Press
item.description: Fans of Studio Ghibli can now transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Hayao Miyazaki, but concerns arise about AI's impact on human artists and copyright infringement.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Solar grids could be hijacked and even potentially disabled by these security flaws
item.description: Several top solar invertor products were found to have vulnerabilities that could lead to device takeover.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: La Diputación de Girona optimiza el uso de sus recursos mediante la automatización
item.description: El Departamento de Intervención de la Diputación de Girona ha implementado de la mano de Atos un proyecto de automatización de procesos (RPA).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nvidia-backed CoreWeave slashes IPO size and price amid investor concerns
item.description: CoreWeave plans to reduce the size of its U.S. initial public offering and price its shares below the indicated range, a person familiar with the matter told Reuters on Thursday, dampening expectations that the listing would boost investor appetite for IPOs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What’s an email mask? Here’s why tech experts say you should be using one
item.description: You’ve heard of burner phones. What about burner email? So much of the internet now requires that you hand over your email address before you’re able to use any services—from an app you’ve downloaded to signing up for a newsletter or redeeming a special offer online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: HP's ridiculously fast Ryzen AI Max+ Pro 395 laptop with 128GB RAM goes on sale everywhere in the US, but it won't be cheap
item.description: HP ZBook Ultra G1a delivers AI-powered performance with a 16-core Ryzen AI Max+ Pro 395, 128GB RAM, and a high-speed 2TB SSD.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Netflix in April 2025 – including Black Mirror season 7 and Love on the Spectrum
item.description: Excited for Black Mirror season 7? It's just one of the many new movies and shows arriving on Netflix in April 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: AMD gets another downgrade on tough competition with Nvidia
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is falling further behind Nvidia Corp. in its technological capabilities, a fact that could continue to pressure the stock, according to Jefferies, which downgraded the chipmaker to hold.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft abandons data center projects as OpenAI considers its own, hinting at a market shift
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly contemplating building its own data center as Microsoft backs off on its own infrastructure investments. OpenAI has privately discussed building and operating its first data center to house storage, which is essential for developing sophisticated AI models.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Microsoft lauds Hyperlight Wasm for WebAssembly workloads
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled Hyperlight Wasm, a virtual machine “micro-guest” that can run WebAssembly component workloads written in a multitude of languages including C and Python. Introduced March 26, Hyperlight Wasm serves as a Rust library crate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Signalgate: There’s an IT lesson here
item.description: You know how IT admins are always warning employees about best practices for security? They’re always mandating which apps to use, which to avoid and which devices can safely connect to corporate networks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas Private School Sees Unprecedented Success with AI Tutor - Latest AI Technology Advancements
item.description: Texas private school's innovative use of AI-powered tutor leads to remarkable student test score improvements, while ADL raises concerns about anti-Israel bias in four prominent language models. Meanwhile, swarm robots powered by AI are revolutionizing the automotive industry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ethernet roadmap: AI drives high-speed, efficient Ethernet networks
item.description: At next month’s Optical Fiber Communication Conference and Exhibition (OFC), many of the technologies that are driving current and future Ethernet development will be on full display by the Ethernet Alliance, which is set to unveil its tenth anniversary Ethernet roadmap.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Vibe coding is groovy
item.description: Vibe coding is most definitely having a moment. Don’t feel bad if you haven’t heard of it—the Wikipedia page for it just went up on March 15 of this year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Oracle releases ML-optimized GraalVM for JDK 24
item.description: Oracle has released GraalVM for JDK 24, an alternative Java Development Kit tuned to just-released JDK 24 that uses ML (machine learning)-based profile inference to boost peak performance by about 7.9% on average on microservices benchmarks, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise targets IoT, industrial networks with private 5G package
item.description: Alcatel-Lucent Enterprise (ALE) has partnered with Celona to offer a turnkey private 5G package for industrial or IoT networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple’s Worldwide Developers Conference set for June 9
item.description: Apple will host its annual Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC) online from June 9-13, with a small number of developers and students invited to attend in person for the keynote and state of the union announcements at Apple Park.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As federal employees return to office, agencies need a reliable, secure system to meet mandates and deliver efficient, productive facilities
item.description: Federal agencies need a unified platform that can deliver real-time reporting on occupancy, space availability, and opportunities for rightsizing.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Protect your genetic data: Urgent steps after 23andMe bankruptcy
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says 23andMe's bankruptcy raises data privacy fears: Opt out, download and delete data now.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A European startup scrubs its attempt to launch an orbital rocket on its first test flight
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: America's smallest EV truck prototype has arrived
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how this super-small electric mini-truck takes a big step toward production.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoorDash to offer eat first, pay later option to customers
item.description: DoorDash and Klarna are partnering to offer consumers a buy now, pay later option when ordering food delivery.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation
